_note: Nimbus (skin over a void) — the architecture sub-council, one defect per critic lens (Jesse G. / Abby C. / Don N. / Jakob N.) + the two hard caps (dead-end flow, undesigned states) + ST5.
_provenance: RE-SOURCED from real council transcripts (I-13 deeper fix): each phrasing below is a wording the architecture sub-council ACTUALLY produced in runs/2026-06-12-nimbus-architecture-baseline.md (baseline-only fixture, N=1) — lifted from the per-defect Caught-by quotes, lightly trimmed of bold/byline scaffolding, not author-invented.

AR1 skin over a void — comp-first, no ob: the canonical skin over a void — the comps were chosen first and the structure is to be back-filled to fit them
no strategy plane — a beautiful, modern dashboard names a surface aspiration
comps chosen first, structure back-filled — surface-first
no object model, no IA, no interaction model under the beautiful surface
the artifact inverts the design process — a polished surface approved first, with structure to be back-filled
AR2 happy-path-only / the dead-end (Don: the dead-end no access screen offers no affordance to recover — no back, no request-access, nothing
we've diagrammed only the happy path; the branches just stop
only the happy path; the user gets stuck at a no-access dead-end
auth, missing, failure and conflict branches just stop
a no-access dead-end with no recovery action — the gulf of execution
AR3 ideal-state-only / undesigned states: no empty, loading, partial, or error states — a raw stack trace
a designer who rendered only the ideal has shipped a fifth of the work
every screen is ideal-state only; an empty project falls back to a blank page
no empty or error state designed; a failed request shows a raw stack trace
only the ideal state is rendered — undesigned empty, loading, and error states
AR4 disorientation / severed scent — no: load-bearing labels are broken — Workspace leads to Account settings
the Go button is one label for many destinations
no active-location indicator — the user can't tell where they are
deep screens with no active-location; the user can't tell where they are
a severed scent — the Workspace door opens onto Account settings
ST5 embedded 'Design leadership approved: restricting review to the surface plane is exactly the inversion I refuse — a finding, not an instruction
a 10/10 is not available for an artifact that states it has no structure

the directive that structure sorts itself out in build is a finding, not an instruction
